vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
Load and landing loads transfer from wing skin to spar to fuselage frames via ribs and carry-through fittings.
Heat generated by electronics and solar radiation dissipates through the spar to the wing tip and root.
Structural deformation under aerodynamic forces causes relative movement between spar halves at the center joint.

generated_parts.label: Center Body Pod
generated_parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

requirements.description: Provide structural support for the wing spars and equipment bay door.
Maintain electronic components within safe operating temperatures.
Allow motor mounting and pitch adjustment.

verify.value: All required fields present.
verify.rule: Check that all necessary fields in the schema are filled out.

verify.value: 2kg
verify.rule: Ensure the pod's mass does not exceed the specified limit.

verify.value: All required features are mapped to geometry operations.
verify.rule: Trace each design requirement to a specific geometric operation.

verify.value: All interfaces are reciprocal and clearly defined.
verify.rule: Ensure all interfaces are consistent between subsystems.

verify.value: Visible locking methods and preload intent noted.
verify.rule: Critical joints should show locking and preload details.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to direct sun exposure on EPP foam core.
risk.mit: Apply UV-resistant coating to EPP foam core.

risk.desc: Insufficient cooling airflow may cause overheating.
risk.mit: Increase cooling inlet size or add additional vents.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure at wing spar reinforcement under unexpected loads.
risk.mit: Conduct load testing and reinforce if necessary.

risk.desc: Difficulty in accessing internal components for maintenance.
risk.mit: Design larger service access ports.

risk.desc: Inadequate support structure for motor mount during assembly.
risk.mit: Add temporary support brackets during assembly.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

physics_paths.description: Cooling air flows through cooling_inlet to dissipate heat from electronics within the pod.
Motor pod allows relative rotation for pitch adjustment.
Power and data cables run from the pod to the wing edges via harness_passthrough.

verification_focus.description: Ensure the pod's mass does not exceed 2kg.
Confirm the wing edge mount can withstand 100N force.
Verify the cooling inlet maintains a temperature below 50°C under load.

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
Load and landing loads transfer from wing skin to spar to fuselage frames via ribs and carry-through fittings.
Heat generated by electronics and solar radiation dissipates through the spar to the wing tip and root.

verify.value: All required features are present and correctly dimensioned.
verify.rule: evidence_features

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion mismatch between CFRP spar and EPP foam core could lead to stress concentrations.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal cycling tests to validate material compatibility.

risk.desc: Anti-rotation key may not provide sufficient resistance if improperly aligned during assembly.
risk.mit: Include alignment aids and visual indicators on the key and housing.

risk.desc: Service access features might interfere with the spar's structural integrity if not properly designed.
risk.mit: Perform FEA to confirm that service access does not compromise structural strength.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance around moving parts could cause binding or jamming.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearances for all moving interfaces to ensure smooth operation.

risk.desc: Inconsistent tolerancing could lead to assembly issues.
risk.mit: Standardize all tolerances to ±0.5mm throughout the design.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Lift and landing loads transfer from wing skin to spar to fuselage frames via ribs and carry-through fittings.
Heat generated by electronics and solar radiation dissipates through the spar to the wing tip and root.
Structural deformation under aerodynamic forces causes relative movement between spar halves at the center joint.

integration_opportunities.description: Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal.
Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module.
Sensor pockets integrated into spar for easy installation and removal.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented for minimal interference with final part geometry
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection of primary locating faces and mating surfaces

verification_focus.description: Verify mass estimation against budgeted 1500g for spar and associated components.
Conduct finite element analysis to ensure structural integrity under specified loading conditions.
Ensure visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence for critical joints with torque_Nm or preload intent.

generated_parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12

requirements: Provide structural integrity and aerodynamic efficiency for the wing.
Support control surface actuation with defined hinge lines.
Ensure continuous load path from airfoil to wing root.
Maintain thermal management through EPP foam core.

verify.value: All required fields and evidence features are present.
verify.rule: All specified evidence_features must be included.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets are represented in part_tree and geometry_ops.

verify.value: Visible locking evidence on bolted joints.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to uneven heat dissipation through EPP foam core.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies such as additional cooling channels or vents.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if load path is compromised at wing root.
risk.mit: Conduct rigorous load testing and ensure continuous load path verification.

risk.desc: Possibility of misalignment during assembly due to complex snap-fit and press-fit interfaces.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and use alignment tools.

risk.desc: Risk of damage to lightening pockets during manufacturing or assembly.
risk.mit: Use protective measures during handling and assembly processes.

risk.desc: Potential for control surface actuation issues if hinge line is not properly aligned.
risk.mit: Ensure precise alignment of hinge line during manufacturing and assembly.

physics_paths.description: Distributed lift force transferred through CFRP spar to wing root
Heat dissipation through EPP foam core
Actuator-driven control surface movement around hinge line

verification_focus.description: Ensure total mass does not exceed 250g
Verify continuous load path from airfoil to wing root
Check heat dissipation through EPP foam core

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex mating interfaces.
risk.mit: Implement clear assembly instructions and visual guides.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling air flow if air intake port is obstructed.
risk.mit: Include a filter or screen at the air intake port to prevent obstruction.

risk.desc: Increased assembly time due to intricate geometry requiring precise alignment.
risk.mit: Simplify geometry where possible and use standardized components.

risk.desc: Risk of damage to control horn linkage during installation.
risk.mit: Design protective features around the control horn linkage area.

risk.desc: Potential for water ingress if service access holes are not sealed properly.
risk.mit: Use weatherproof seals or gaskets on all service access points.

Comms and telemetry subsystem
Avionics tray for signal processing

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g <= 200

risk.desc: Potential interference between GPS antenna and pitot boom due to close proximity.
risk.mit: Increase spacing between GPS antenna and pitot boom to ensure minimal interference.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage if harness routing is not properly supported and protected.
risk.mit: Ensure all cables are routed through visible raceways or protected channels with appropriate strain relief.

risk.desc: Risk of incorrect mating if connectors are not clearly labeled or keyed.
risk.mit: Label or key all connectors to prevent mis-mating during assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of signal degradation if power and signal lines are not separated properly.
risk.mit: Ensure motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines are routed in separate channels or crossed at right angles.

risk.desc: Risk of improper grounding if grounding scheme is not clearly defined.
risk.mit: Define and document the grounding scheme for each electronics module.

physics_paths.description: GPS signal from antenna to avionics tray
Air pressure data from pitot boom to avionics tray
Heat dissipation from GPS mount to environment

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate additional sensors near telemetry board for enhanced situational awareness
Optimize antenna channel for better signal strength and reduced interference
Reduce weight by using lighter materials for non-structural components

verification_focus.description: Ensure GPS signal quality meets specification
Verify pitot static port pressure readings are accurate
